title: jatropha gossypiifolia var. staphysagriifolia
intro: Jatropha gossypiifolia var. staphysagriifolia is a plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family, commonly known as the velvet leaf or velvet-leaf staphisagria. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its purgative and laxative properties, which are attributed to its latex and alkaloid content. The medicinal properties of Jatropha gossypiifolia var. staphysagriifolia include treating constipation, indigestion, and other gastrointestinal issues, making it beneficial for overall gut health. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by boiling the leaves and roots in water or using them in decoctions to create teas and infusions for medicinal purposes.
